title: E. coli Plating Quantification
author: Kenneth Schackart
license_note: Confirmed via CrossRef DOI metadata: dx.doi.org/10.17504/protocols.io.xqhfmt6

steps.title: Transfer 100 µL of stock solution...
steps.instruction: Transfer 100 µL of stock solution to 2 mL centrifuge tube.

steps.title: Add 900 µL DI water to...
steps.instruction: Add 900 µL DI water to the same centrifuge tube to make a dilution that is one-tenth the concentration of the stock solution.

steps.title: Using 100 µL of the dilution...
steps.instruction: Using 100 µL of the dilution you just made, make another dilution that is one-tenth the concentration of the second solution.

steps.title: Repeat 6 times until you have...
steps.instruction: Repeat 6 times until you have a solution that is diluted by 10 6 . How to perform serial dilutions. X represents the number of 10-fold dilutions performed at each point.

steps.title: Choose a dilution that you feel...
steps.instruction: Choose a dilution that you feel is appropriate and dispense 100 µL of that dilution on agar plate.

steps.title: Evenly spread the cell suspension over...
steps.instruction: Evenly spread the cell suspension over the agarose gel with inoculation loop. This may be different from how you have streaked before. We are wanting the solution evenly spread so that we can count how many colonies are formed after incubation.

steps.title: Wrap the agar plate's edge in parafilm
steps.instruction: Wrap the agar plate's edge in parafilm. If you have not worked with Parafilm, ask a TA for help in wrapping your plate.

steps.title: Wait 2 minutes, then flip the plate upside-down for incubation
steps.instruction: Wait 2 minutes, then flip the plate upside-down for incubation.

steps.title: Label the plate with
steps.instruction: Label the plate with: Group number Date Dilution used (10^x)

steps.title: Incubate at 37 °C over night
steps.instruction: Incubate at 37 °C over night. Performed by T.A.

steps.title: Take image of incubated plate
steps.instruction: Take image of incubated plate. Performed by T.A. Include this image in the lab report results section.

steps.title: Using the image of your plate,...
steps.instruction: Using the image of your plate, count the number of colonies formed. This can be done by hand or using an image processing software such as ImageJ.

steps.title: Calculate the original E
steps.instruction: Calculate the original E. Coli concentration using the following equation: Include the result of this calculation in the lab report.
